Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF PAUL DRAKE's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

1. Plaintiffs' motion to compel the deposition of the defendant's person most knowledgeable and production of documents/Granted.
2. Defendant must produce the PMK and all-nonprivileged responsive documents by April by 25, 2025.
3. If any documents are withheld on the grounds of privilege, the withheld documents must be identified in a privilege log.
4. The discovery sought by plaintiffs is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and the defendant's boilerplate objections lack merit.
5. Plaintiff's "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects is proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].)
6. Defendant did not present any competent evidence showing that the requests are unduly burdensome.
7. Defendant did not act with substantial justification and shall pay $2,000 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 10 days of service of service of order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/BZ)
